toolUseResult.content:
"""Advanced ICT / Smart Money signals: MSS, CHoCH, sweeps, OTE, IPDA, SMT, PO3."""
from typing import Optional
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import pytz

NY_TZ = pytz.timezone("America/New_York")


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Swing point detection
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def detect_swings(bars: list, lookback: int = 3) -> dict:
    """Identify pivot highs and pivot lows."""
    highs, lows = [], []
    n = len(bars)
    for i in range(lookback, n - lookback):
        h = bars[i].get("high")
        l = bars[i].get("low")
        if h is None or l is None:
            continue
        # Swing high: highest in window
        window_highs = [bars[j].get("high", 0) for j in range(i - lookback, i + lookback + 1) if j != i]
        if all(h >= wh for wh in window_highs if wh):
            highs.append({"index": i, "price": round(h, 2), "time": bars[i].get("time")})
        # Swing low
        window_lows = [bars[j].get("low", float("inf")) for j in range(i - lookback, i + lookback + 1) if j != i]
        if all(l <= wl for wl in window_lows if wl != float("inf")):
            lows.append({"index": i, "price": round(l, 2), "time": bars[i].get("time")})
    return {"swing_highs": highs[-10:], "swing_lows": lows[-10:]}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# MSS / CHoCH detection
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def detect_mss_choch(bars: list, swings: Optional[dict] = None) -> dict:
    """
    Detect Market Structure Shift and Change of Character.

    CHoCH: price closes beyond a recent swing in opposite direction (lower TF signal)
    MSS: CHoCH confirmed with strong displacement (higher confidence reversal)
    """
    if swings is None:
        swings = detect_swings(bars)

    swing_highs = swings.get("swing_highs", [])
    swing_lows = swings.get("swing_lows", [])
    events = []

    if len(bars) < 5 or not swing_highs or not swing_lows:
        return {"events": events, "last_structure": "unknown", "bias": "neutral"}

    # Look at the most recent swing high and low
    last_high = swing_highs[-1] if swing_highs else None
    last_low = swing_lows[-1] if swing_lows else None

    # Check recent bars for structure breaks
    for i in range(max(1, len(bars) - 20), len(bars)):
        b = bars[i]
        close = b.get("close")
        if close is None:
            continue

        # Bearish CHoCH: close below the most recent swing low
        if last_low and close < last_low["price"] and i > last_low["index"]:
            prev_bar = bars[i - 1]
            displacement = abs(close - (prev_bar.get("open") or close))
            is_mss = displacement > 0.3  # strong displacement = MSS
            events.append({
                "type": "MSS_bearish" if is_mss else "CHoCH_bearish",
                "label": "MSS ↓" if is_mss else "CHoCH ↓",
                "price": round(close, 2),
                "broken_level": last_low["price"],
                "time": b.get("time"),
                "description": (
                    f"Bearish MSS — strong close below swing low {last_low['price']:.2f}. "
                    f"Structure shifted bearish." if is_mss else
                    f"Bearish CHoCH — close below swing low {last_low['price']:.2f}. "
                    f"Watch for distribution."
                ),
                "color": "red",
            })

        # Bullish CHoCH: close above the most recent swing high
        if last_high and close > last_high["price"] and i > last_high["index"]:
            prev_bar = bars[i - 1]
            displacement = abs(close - (prev_bar.get("open") or close))
            is_mss = displacement > 0.3
            events.append({
                "type": "MSS_bullish" if is_mss else "CHoCH_bullish",
                "label": "MSS ↑" if is_mss else "CHoCH ↑",
                "price": round(close, 2),
                "broken_level": last_high["price"],
                "time": b.get("time"),
                "description": (
                    f"Bullish MSS — strong close above swing high {last_high['price']:.2f}. "
                    f"Structure shifted bullish." if is_mss else
                    f"Bullish CHoCH — close above swing high {last_high['price']:.2f}. "
                    f"Watch for accumulation."
                ),
                "color": "green",
            })

    # Overall structure bias from last few swings
    last_structure = "neutral"
    if len(swing_highs) >= 2 and len(swing_lows) >= 2:
        hh = swing_highs[-1]["price"] > swing_highs[-2]["price"]
        hl = swing_lows[-1]["price"] > swing_lows[-2]["price"]
        lh = swing_highs[-1]["price"] < swing_highs[-2]["price"]
        ll = swing_lows[-1]["price"] < swing_lows[-2]["price"]
        if hh and hl:
            last_structure = "bullish"
        elif lh and ll:
            last_structure = "bearish"
        elif hh or hl:
            last_structure = "bullish_lean"
        elif lh or ll:
            last_structure = "bearish_lean"

    return {
        "events": events[-5:],
        "last_structure": last_structure,
        "bias": last_structure,
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Liquidity sweep detector
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def detect_liquidity_sweeps(bars: list, session_levels: dict, equal_hl: Optional[dict] = None) -> list:
    """
    Detect when price wicked through a key level but closed back inside (sweep/stop hunt).
    """
    sweeps = []
    if len(bars) < 3:
        return sweeps

    # Build level list to check
    levels = []
    for key, label in [
        ("asia_high", "Asia High"), ("asia_low", "Asia Low"),
        ("london_high", "London High"), ("london_low", "London Low"),
        ("premarket_high", "Pre-Market High"), ("premarket_low", "Pre-Market Low"),
        ("prev_day_high", "Prev Day High"), ("prev_day_low", "Prev Day Low"),
        ("today_high", "Today's High"), ("today_low", "Today's Low"),
    ]:
        v = session_levels.get(key)
        if v:
            levels.append({"level": v, "label": label, "is_high": "high" in key})

    if equal_hl:
        for eh in equal_hl.get("equal_highs", []):
            levels.append({"level": eh["level"], "label": f"EQH {eh['level']:.2f}", "is_high": True})
        for el in equal_hl.get("equal_lows", []):
            levels.append({"level": el["level"], "label": f"EQL {el['level']:.2f}", "is_high": False})

    # Check recent bars for wick-through + body-close-back
    for i in range(max(1, len(bars) - 30), len(bars)):
        b = bars[i]
        high, low = b.get("high"), b.get("low")
        open_, close = b.get("open"), b.get("close")
        if None in (high, low, open_, close):
            continue

        body_high = max(open_, close)
        body_low = min(open_, close)

        for lvl in levels:
            lv = lvl["level"]
            label = lvl["label"]
            is_high_level = lvl["is_high"]

            # Bullish sweep: wick below level, body closes above
            if is_high_level is False and low < lv and body_low >= lv:
                sweeps.append({
                    "type": "bullish_sweep",
                    "label": f"Swept {label}",
                    "level": lv,
                    "wick_low": round(low, 2),
                    "close": round(close, 2),
                    "time": b.get("time"),
                    "description": (
                        f"Bullish sweep of {label} ({lv:.2f}) — wick below, closed above. "
                        f"Stop-hunt complete. Look for bullish iFVG entry targeting higher draws."
                    ),
                    "direction": "bullish",
                })

            # Bearish sweep: wick above level, body closes below
            if is_high_level is True and high > lv and body_high <= lv:
                sweeps.append({
                    "type": "bearish_sweep",
                    "label": f"Swept {label}",
                    "level": lv,
                    "wick_high": round(high, 2),
                    "close": round(close, 2),
                    "time": b.get("time"),
                    "description": (
                        f"Bearish sweep of {label} ({lv:.2f}) — wick above, closed below. "
                        f"Stop-hunt complete. Look for bearish iFVG entry targeting lower draws."
                    ),
                    "direction": "bearish",
                })

    return sweeps[-10:]


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# OTE Zone (Optimal Trade Entry 0.618–0.786 Fib)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def calc_ote_zone(bars: list, swings: Optional[dict] = None, direction: str = "bullish") -> Optional[dict]:
    """
    Calculate the OTE retracement zone from the most recent impulse leg.

    For a bullish setup: measure from the sweep low to the subsequent high.
    OTE = 0.618 to 0.786 retracement into that leg (discount zone for entry).
    """
    if swings is None:
        swings = detect_swings(bars)

    highs = swings.get("swing_highs", [])
    lows = swings.get("swing_lows", [])

    if not highs or not lows:
        return None

    if direction == "bullish":
        # Impulse: from most recent low to most recent high (after that low)
        anchor_low = lows[-1]
        # Find highest high AFTER the anchor low
        highs_after = [h for h in highs if h["index"] > anchor_low["index"]]
        if not highs_after:
            return None
        anchor_high = max(highs_after, key=lambda x: x["price"])
        leg_low, leg_high = anchor_low["price"], anchor_high["price"]
        leg_range = leg_high - leg_low
        if leg_range <= 0:
            return None
        ote_top = round(leg_high - 0.618 * leg_range, 2)   # 0.618 retrace
        ote_bot = round(leg_high - 0.786 * leg_range, 2)   # 0.786 retrace
        eq = round((ote_top + ote_bot) / 2, 2)
        return {
            "direction": "bullish",
            "leg_high": round(leg_high, 2),
            "leg_low": round(leg_low, 2),
            "ote_top": ote_top,
            "ote_bottom": ote_bot,
            "equilibrium": eq,
            "description": (
                f"Bullish OTE {ote_bot:.2f}–{ote_top:.2f} (0.786–0.618 retrace of "
                f"{leg_low:.2f}→{leg_high:.2f}). Price entering this zone = optimal long entry."
            ),
        }
    else:
        anchor_high = highs[-1]
        lows_after = [l for l in lows if l["index"] > anchor_high["index"]]
        if not lows_after:
            return None
        anchor_low = min(lows_after, key=lambda x: x["price"])
        leg_high, leg_low = anchor_high["price"], anchor_low["price"]
        leg_range = leg_high - leg_low
        if leg_range <= 0:
            return None
        ote_bot = round(leg_low + 0.618 * leg_range, 2)
        ote_top = round(leg_low + 0.786 * leg_range, 2)
        eq = round((ote_top + ote_bot) / 2, 2)
        return {
            "direction": "bearish",
            "leg_high": round(leg_high, 2),
            "leg_low": round(leg_low, 2),
            "ote_top": ote_top,
            "ote_bottom": ote_bot,
            "equilibrium": eq,
            "description": (
                f"Bearish OTE {ote_bot:.2f}–{ote_top:.2f} (0.618–0.786 retrace of "
                f"{leg_high:.2f}→{leg_low:.2f}). Price entering this zone = optimal short entry."
            ),
        }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# IPDA Levels (20 / 40 / 60 day rolling highs and lows)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def calc_ipda_levels(bars: list) -> dict:
    """
    IPDA draw levels: 20, 40, 60 trading-day rolling highs and lows.
    Approximated from available intraday bars (5m → group by day).
    """
    if not bars:
        return {}

    # Group bars by trading date (ET)
    days: dict = {}
    for b in bars:
        t_raw = b.get("time", "")
        try:
            if t_raw.endswith("Z"):
                t_raw = t_raw[:-1] + "+00:00"
            dt_et = datetime.fromisoformat(t_raw).astimezone(NY_TZ)
            day_key = dt_et.date().isoformat()
        except Exception:
            continue
        h, l = b.get("high"), b.get("low")
        if h is None or l is None:
            continue
        if day_key not in days:
            days[day_key] = {"high": h, "low": l}
        else:
            days[day_key]["high"] = max(days[day_key]["high"], h)
            days[day_key]["low"] = min(days[day_key]["low"], l)

    sorted_days = sorted(days.keys())
    result = {}
    for period in [20, 40, 60]:
        recent = sorted_days[-period:] if len(sorted_days) >= period else sorted_days
        if not recent:
            continue
        period_high = max(days[d]["high"] for d in recent)
        period_low = min(days[d]["low"] for d in recent)
        result[f"ipda_{period}d_high"] = round(period_high, 2)
        result[f"ipda_{period}d_low"] = round(period_low, 2)
        result[f"ipda_{period}d_range"] = round(period_high - period_low, 2)

    return result


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# SMT Divergence (QQQ vs SPY non-confirmation)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def detect_smt_divergence(
    bars_primary: list,
    bars_secondary: list,
    lookback: int = 10,
) -> dict:
    """
    Detect SMT divergence between two correlated instruments.

    Bearish SMT: primary makes higher high, secondary does NOT confirm.
    Bullish SMT: primary makes lower low, secondary does NOT confirm.
    """
    if len(bars_primary) < lookback + 2 or len(bars_secondary) < lookback + 2:
        return {"detected": False, "type": None, "description": "Insufficient data for SMT"}

    def recent_extremes(bars, n):
        recent = bars[-n:]
        highs = [b.get("high") for b in recent if b.get("high")]
        lows = [b.get("low") for b in recent if b.get("low")]
        return (max(highs) if highs else None), (min(lows) if lows else None)

    p_high, p_low = recent_extremes(bars_primary, lookback)
    s_high, s_low = recent_extremes(bars_secondary, lookback)
    pp_high, pp_low = recent_extremes(bars_primary, lookback * 2)
    ps_high, ps_low = recent_extremes(bars_secondary, lookback * 2)

    if None in (p_high, p_low, s_high, s_low, pp_high, pp_low, ps_high, ps_low):
        return {"detected": False, "type": None, "description": "Missing price data"}

    # Bearish SMT: primary new high, secondary fails to confirm
    bearish_smt = (p_high > pp_high) and (s_high < ps_high)
    # Bullish SMT: primary new low, secondary fails to confirm
    bullish_smt = (p_low < pp_low) and (s_low > ps_low)

    if bearish_smt:
        return {
            "detected": True,
            "type": "bearish_smt",
            "label": "Bearish SMT",
            "description": (
                f"Bearish SMT divergence — primary made new high ({p_high:.2f}) "
                f"but secondary failed to confirm. Weakness signal. "
                f"Watch for reversal / bearish iFVG setup."
            ),
            "color": "red",
        }
    if bullish_smt:
        return {
            "detected": True,
            "type": "bullish_smt",
            "label": "Bullish SMT",
            "description": (
                f"Bullish SMT divergence — primary made new low ({p_low:.2f}) "
                f"but secondary failed to confirm. Strength signal. "
                f"Watch for reversal / bullish iFVG setup."
            ),
            "color": "green",
        }

    return {"detected": False, "type": None, "description": "No SMT divergence detected"}


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PO3 / AMD Phase (Accumulation → Manipulation → Distribution)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def detect_po3_phase(bars: list, session_levels: dict) -> dict:
    """
    Detect which AMD phase the current NY session is in.

    Accumulation: low volatility range before sweep
    Manipulation: sweep of a key level (stop hunt)
    Distribution: price delivers away from the sweep toward the target
    """
    if not bars or not session_levels:
        return {"phase": "unknown", "description": "Insufficient data"}

    now_et = datetime.now(NY_TZ)
    session_open_m = 9 * 60 + 30
    current_m = now_et.hour * 60 + now_et.minute
    minutes_since_open = current_m - session_open_m

    # Get today's bars only (from 9:30 onward)
    today_et = now_et.date()
    today_bars = []
    for b in bars:
        t_raw = b.get("time", "")
        try:
            if t_raw.endswith("Z"):
                t_raw = t_raw[:-1] + "+00:00"
            dt_et = datetime.fromisoformat(t_raw).astimezone(NY_TZ)
            bar_m = dt_et.hour * 60 + dt_et.minute
            if dt_et.date() == today_et and bar_m >= session_open_m:
                today_bars.append(b)
        except Exception:
            continue

    if not today_bars:
        return {"phase": "pre_market", "description": "Market not yet open or no today bars"}

    if minutes_since_open < 0:
        return {"phase": "pre_market", "description": "Pre-market: mark your levels, don't trade yet"}

    today_highs = [b.get("high") for b in today_bars if b.get("high")]
    today_lows = [b.get("low") for b in today_bars if b.get("low")]
    today_closes = [b.get("close") for b in today_bars if b.get("close")]

    if not today_highs or not today_lows:
        return {"phase": "accumulation", "description": "Waiting for opening range to form"}

    session_high = max(today_highs)
    session_low = min(today_lows)
    current_price = today_closes[-1] if today_closes else None
    session_range = session_high - session_low

    # Check if a key level was swept
    key_levels = []
    for key in ["asia_high", "asia_low", "london_high", "london_low", "prev_day_high", "prev_day_low"]:
        v = session_levels.get(key)
        if v:
            key_levels.append(v)

    swept_level = None
    for lv in key_levels:
        if session_low < lv < session_high:
            swept_level = lv
            break

    # Determine phase based on time + price action
    if minutes_since_open <= 15:
        phase = "accumulation"
        desc = "Opening range forming (first 15 min). Mark highs/lows — do NOT trade yet."
    elif swept_level and session_range < 1.5 and minutes_since_open <= 45:
        phase = "manipulation"
        desc = (
            f"Manipulation phase — price swept level at {swept_level:.2f}. "
            f"Watch for iFVG forming. If price displaces away, distribution may begin."
        )
    elif swept_level and current_price and session_range >= 1.5:
        # Price moved away from sweep
        if current_price > swept_level:
            phase = "distribution_bullish"
            desc = (
                f"Distribution (bullish) — price swept {swept_level:.2f} low, now delivering UP. "
                f"Longs valid. Look for iFVG re-entries on pullbacks."
            )
        else:
            phase = "distribution_bearish"
            desc = (
                f"Distribution (bearish) — price swept {swept_level:.2f} high, now delivering DOWN. "
                f"Shorts valid. Look for iFVG re-entries on retests."
            )
    elif minutes_since_open <= 45:
        phase = "manipulation"
        desc = (
            f"Manipulation phase — waiting for a key level to be swept. "
            f"Key levels: {', '.join(f'{l:.2f}' for l in key_levels[:4])}."
        )
    else:
        phase = "late_session"
        desc = "Past prime window. Only take A+ setups. Reduce size."

    return {
        "phase": phase,
        "phase_label": {
            "accumulation": "Accumulation",
            "manipulation": "Manipulation",
            "distribution_bullish": "Distribution ↑",
            "distribution_bearish": "Distribution ↓",
            "late_session": "Late Session",
            "pre_market": "Pre-Market",
        }.get(phase, phase.replace("_", " ").title()),
        "minutes_since_open": minutes_since_open,
        "swept_level": swept_level,
        "session_range": round(session_range, 2),
        "description": desc,
    }


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Combined advanced analysis
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

def get_advanced_signals(
    bars: list,
    session_levels: dict,
    equal_hl: Optional[dict] = None,
    bars_secondary: Optional[list] = None,  # e.g. SPY bars for SMT
    bias_direction: str = "neutral",
) -> dict:
    """Run all advanced ICT signals and return combined result."""
    swings = detect_swings(bars)
    mss = detect_mss_choch(bars, swings)
    sweeps = detect_liquidity_sweeps(bars, session_levels, equal_hl)
    po3 = detect_po3_phase(bars, session_levels)
    ipda = calc_ipda_levels(bars)

    # OTE: use bias direction to determine which leg to measure
    ote_direction = "bullish" if bias_direction in ("bullish", "bullish_lean") else "bearish"
    ote = calc_ote_zone(bars, swings, direction=ote_direction)

    # SMT only if secondary bars provided
    smt = {"detected": False, "type": None, "description": "No secondary instrument"}
    if bars_secondary:
        smt = detect_smt_divergence(bars, bars_secondary)

    # Current price
    current_price = None
    for b in reversed(bars):
        if b.get("close"):
            current_price = b["close"]
            break

    # Price in OTE?
    in_ote = False
    if ote and current_price:
        in_ote = ote["ote_bottom"] <= current_price <= ote["ote_top"]

    # Recent sweep (last 10 bars)?
    recent_sweep = sweeps[-1] if sweeps else None
    has_recent_sweep = bool(recent_sweep)

    # Setup quality boost from advanced signals
    advanced_score_bonus = 0
    if has_recent_sweep:
        advanced_score_bonus += 15
    if in_ote:
        advanced_score_bonus += 10
    if smt.get("detected"):
        advanced_score_bonus += 10
    if mss.get("last_structure") in ("bullish", "bearish"):
        advanced_score_bonus += 5

    return {
        "swing_points": swings,
        "mss_choch": mss,
        "liquidity_sweeps": sweeps,
        "ote_zone": ote,
        "in_ote": in_ote,
        "ipda_levels": ipda,
        "smt_divergence": smt,
        "po3_phase": po3,
        "advanced_score_bonus": advanced_score_bonus,
        "current_price": current_price,
        "has_recent_sweep": has_recent_sweep,
        "recent_sweep": recent_sweep,
        "summary": {
            "structure": mss.get("last_structure", "unknown"),
            "phase": po3.get("phase_label", "unknown"),
            "sweep_detected": has_recent_sweep,
            "in_ote": in_ote,
            "smt": smt.get("type"),
        }
    }

"""Risk management API routes — prop firm math + position sizing."""
from fastapi import APIRouter, Query
from engines.risk import calc_account_status, size_position, daily_trade_plan, INSTRUMENTS

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/risk", tags=["risk"])


@router.get("/account")
async def account_status(
    balance: float = Query(25000.0, description="Current account balance"),
    highest: float = Query(None, description="Session highest balance (defaults to balance)"),
):
    status = calc_account_status(balance, highest)
    plan = daily_trade_plan(status)
    return {**status, "trade_plan": plan}


@router.get("/size")
async def position_size(
    instrument: str = Query("MNQ", description="MNQ, MES, or MGC"),
    stop_points: float = Query(..., description="Stop distance in points"),
    balance: float = Query(25000.0),
    highest: float = Query(None),
    risk_pct: float = Query(0.3, description="Fraction of daily risk remaining to use per trade"),
    max_risk: float = Query(300.0, description="Absolute max risk per trade in dollars"),
):
    status = calc_account_status(balance, highest)
    sizing = size_position(
        instrument=instrument,
        stop_points=stop_points,
        account_status=status,
        risk_per_trade_pct=risk_pct,
        max_risk_per_trade=max_risk,
    )
    return {
        "account": {
            "balance": status["current_balance"],
            "daily_risk_remaining": status["daily_risk_remaining"],
            "status": status["status"],
        },
        "sizing": sizing,
    }


@router.get("/instruments")
async def instruments():
    return {
        k: {
            **v,
            "example_stop_5pt_risk": round(5 * v["dollars_per_point"], 2),
            "example_stop_10pt_risk": round(10 * v["dollars_per_point"], 2),
        }
        for k, v in INSTRUMENTS.items()
    }


@router.get("/payout")
async def payout_status(
    balance: float = Query(25000.0),
    highest: float = Query(None),
):
    status = calc_account_status(balance, highest)
    return {
        "current_balance": status["current_balance"],
        "payout_ready": status["payout_ready"],
        "payout_threshold": status["payout_threshold"],
        "to_threshold": status["to_payout_threshold"],
        "max_payout": status["max_payout"],
        "safe_trigger": status["safe_payout_trigger"],
        "to_safe_trigger": status["to_safe_trigger"],
        "safe_after_payout": status["safe_after_payout"],
        "days_at_300": status["days_at_300"],
        "days_at_400": status["days_at_400"],
        "days_at_500": status["days_at_500"],
        "message": (
            "Ready to take $1,500 payout!" if status["payout_ready"] and status["current_balance"] >= status["safe_payout_trigger"]
            else f"Need ${status['to_safe_trigger']:.0f} more for safe payout trigger"
            if not status["payout_ready"] else
            f"Above threshold — need ${status['to_safe_trigger']:.0f} more for safe payout buffer"
        ),
    }

from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from dotenv import load_dotenv
import uvicorn

load_dotenv()

from database import init_db
from routes.market import router as market_router
from routes.flow import router as flow_router
from routes.tape import router as tape_router
from routes.replay import router as replay_router
from routes.journal import router as journal_router
from routes.providers import router as providers_router

from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
from fastapi.middleware.cors import CORSMiddleware
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from dotenv import load_dotenv
import uvicorn

load_dotenv()

from database import init_db
from routes.market import router as market_router
from routes.flow import router as flow_router
from routes.tape import router as tape_router
from routes.replay import router as replay_router
from routes.journal import router as journal_router
from routes.providers import router as providers_router
from routes.schwab import router as schwab_router
from routes.ict import router as ict_router
from routes.risk import router as risk_router


app = FastAPI(title="NASDAQ Flow Terminal", version="1.0.0")

app.add_middleware(
    CORSMiddleware,
    allow_origins=["*"],
    allow_credentials=True,
    allow_methods=["*"],
    allow_headers=["*"],
)

app.include_router(market_router)
app.include_router(flow_router)
app.include_router(tape_router)
app.include_router(replay_router)
app.include_router(journal_router)
app.include_router(providers_router)
app.include_router(schwab_router)
app.include_router(ict_router)
app.include_router(risk_router)


@app.on_event("startup")
async def startup():
    await init_db()


@app.exception_handler(Exception)
async def global_exception_handler(request: Request, exc: Exception):
    return JSONResponse(status_code=500, content={"error": str(exc), "path": str(request.url)})


@app.get("/api/health")
async def health():
    return {"status": "ok", "version": "1.0.0"}


if __name__ == "__main__":
    uvicorn.run("main:app", host="0.0.0.0", port=8000, reload=True)

---
name: user-profile
description: Diego's trading background, goals, prop firm setup, and how to work with him
metadata:
  type: user
---

Diego is learning to trade futures (MNQ primarily, sometimes MES) using ICT/SMC concepts — specifically the Sweep & Inverse / iFVG model. He is on a Lucid 25k Pro prop firm account. He works at a desktop at home and a work laptop via GitHub Codespaces browser.

**Trading focus:**
- Primary instrument: MNQ (Micro E-mini Nasdaq-100, $2/pt)
- Secondary: MES (Micro E-mini S&P 500, $5/pt)
- Special: MGC (Micro Gold, $10/pt) for Asia session only
- Session: NY Killzone 9:30–11:30 AM ET (MNQ/MES), Asia session for MGC
- Strategy: ICT Sweep & Inverse Model — mark session levels, wait for sweep, find iFVG, enter on inversion targeting opposing draw on liquidity
- Timeframes: 5m + 15m for structure, 1m/2m/3m/30s for entries

**Prop firm (Lucid 25k Pro):**
- Starting balance: $25,000
- Daily loss limit: $1,000 (trailing from session high)
- Floor stops trailing at: $25,100
- Payout threshold: $26,100
- Max payout: $1,500
- Safe payout trigger: $28,000 (leaves $26,500 after $1,500)
- Strategy: ~$300/day to hit safe trigger in ~10 trading days

**Data:**
- Schwab API: credentials in backend/.env (APP_KEY, APP_SECRET, CALLBACK_URL)
- Schwab OAuth: callback URL must match Schwab portal — production updates after market hours
- Yahoo Finance: fallback for all data while Schwab OAuth not yet active
- Schwab changes apply after-hours (not instant on production apps)

**Setup:**
- Home: VS Code desktop connected to GitHub Codespace OR local clone
- Work: GitHub Codespaces browser + Claude Code in separate browser tab
- Must push before leaving each location (`git push origin main`)

input.content:
---
name: project-state
description: Current build status of NASDAQ Flow Terminal — what's done, what's next
metadata:
  type: project
---

**Stack:** FastAPI backend (Python) + React/Vite frontend (Tailwind CSS) + SQLite
**Repo:** github.com/Koestas/nasdaq-flow-terminal, branch: main
**Data:** Yahoo Finance (active fallback) + Schwab API (configured, OAuth pending)

## What's built and committed

**Backend engines:**
- `engines/bias.py` — master bias score -100 to +100 (VWAP, WAVE, leadership, momentum, gamma walls)
- `engines/ict.py` — ICT analysis: FVG/iFVG detection, order blocks, session levels (Asia/London/NY/prev day), equal H/L, discount/premium, draw on liquidity, setup scoring
- `engines/ict_signals.py` — Advanced signals: swing points, MSS/CHoCH, liquidity sweeps, OTE zone (0.618–0.786), IPDA 20/40/60d levels, SMT divergence, PO3/AMD phase detection
- `engines/risk.py` — Lucid 25k Pro prop firm math: trailing floor, position sizer (MNQ/MES/MGC), payout calculator, daily trade plan
- `engines/stats.py` — stats calculations

**Backend routes:**
- market, flow, tape, replay, journal, providers, schwab, ict, risk

**Frontend pages (14 + Risk):**
- Overview, Wave, GEX, TopFlow, Unusual, RawChain, Leadership, Structure, News, Tape, Replay, Journal, Diagnostics, ICT, Risk

**Schwab integration:**
- `providers/schwab.py` — full OAuth2 flow, quotes, options chain, price history, VIX
- Callback URL in .env: `https://urban-space-giggle-56pxvvxr759h7p9g-8000.app.github.dev/api/schwab/callback`
- Portal changes apply after market hours — needs manual update when Codespace URL changes

## What the ICT strategy does (Sweep & Inverse)

1. Mark before open: Asia H/L, London H/L, NY prev day H/L, relative equal H/L, HTF FVGs
2. Wait at open — do nothing until a key level is swept (wick through + body closes back)
3. Find FVG on 30s/1m/2m/3m created during the sweep leg
4. Wait for iFVG: price aggressively displaces back through that gap (V-shape)
5. Must have opposing draw on liquidity as target
6. Location: longs in discount (<50% of daily range), shorts in premium (>50%)
7. NY Killzone only: 9:30–11:30 AM ET (first 45 min ideal)

## What's next / possible next steps

- Wire advanced ICT signals (`ict_signals.py`) into the ICT page UI — currently the page shows basic ICT analysis but not MSS/CHoCH, OTE, IPDA, SMT, PO3 tabs
- Schwab OAuth: update callback URL once Codespace URL is stable, then authenticate at `/api/schwab/login`
- MGC Asia session page/section (Gold trading, Asia killzone 7 PM–3 AM ET)
- Alert system: notify when sweep detected, iFVG inverted, killzone starts
- Backtesting/replay improvements

1	"""ICT / Smart Money analysis routes."""
2	from fastapi import APIRouter, Query
3	from engines.ict import (get_ict_analysis, get_session_context,
4	                          detect_fvg, extract_session_levels,
5	                          calc_discount_premium, identify_draw_on_liquidity,
6	                          detect_equal_highs_lows)
7	from providers import yahoo, schwab as schwab_provider
8	
9	router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/ict", tags=["ict"])
10	
11	
12	async def _get_bars(symbol: str = "QQQ") -> list:
13	    if schwab_provider.has_tokens():
14	        try:
15	            data = await schwab_provider.get_price_history(
16	                symbol, period_type="day", period=2,
17	                frequency_type="minute", frequency=5, extended_hours=True,
18	            )
19	            if data and data.get("candles"):
20	                import datetime
21	                bars = []
22	                for c in data["candles"]:
23	                    dt = datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(c["datetime"] / 1000, tz=datetime.timezone.utc)
24	                    bars.append({"time": dt.isoformat(), "open": c.get("open"),
25	                                 "high": c.get("high"), "low": c.get("low"),
26	                                 "close": c.get("close"), "volume": c.get("volume")})
27	                return bars
28	        except Exception:
29	            pass
30	    return yahoo.get_intraday(symbol, interval="5m")
31	
32	
33	@router.get("/analysis")
34	async def ict_analysis(symbol: str = Query("QQQ")):
35	    bars = await _get_bars(symbol)
36	    vwap = yahoo.get_vwap(symbol)
37	    price = bars[-1]["close"] if bars and bars[-1].get("close") else None
38	    return get_ict_analysis(bars, vwap=vwap, current_price=price)
39	
40	
41	@router.get("/session")
42	async def session_context():
43	    return get_session_context()
44	
45	
46	@router.get("/fvgs")
47	async def fvgs(symbol: str = Query("QQQ")):
48	    bars = await _get_bars(symbol)
49	    return {"symbol": symbol, "fvgs": detect_fvg(bars), "bar_count": len(bars)}
50	
51	
52	@router.get("/levels")
53	async def session_levels(symbol: str = Query("QQQ")):
54	    bars = await _get_bars(symbol)
55	    levels = extract_session_levels(bars)
56	    price = levels.get("current_price")
57	    equal_hl = detect_equal_highs_lows(bars)
58	    return {
59	        "symbol": symbol, "levels": levels,
60	        "discount_premium": calc_discount_premium(levels, price),
61	        "draw_on_liquidity": identify_draw_on_liquidity(levels, equal_hl, price),
62	        "equal_highs_lows": equal_hl,
63	    }
64

input.new_string:
@router.get("/levels")
async def session_levels(symbol: str = Query("QQQ")):
    bars = await _get_bars(symbol)
    levels = extract_session_levels(bars)
    price = levels.get("current_price")
    equal_hl = detect_equal_highs_lows(bars)
    return {
        "symbol": symbol, "levels": levels,
        "discount_premium": calc_discount_premium(levels, price),
        "draw_on_liquidity": identify_draw_on_liquidity(levels, equal_hl, price),
        "equal_highs_lows": equal_hl,
    }


@router.get("/advanced")
async def advanced_analysis(symbol: str = Query("QQQ"), secondary: str = Query("SPY")):
    """Advanced ICT signals: MSS/CHoCH, sweeps, OTE, IPDA, SMT, PO3."""
    bars = await _get_bars(symbol)
    bars_secondary = await _get_bars(secondary)

    session_levels = extract_session_levels(bars)
    equal_hl = detect_equal_highs_lows(bars)
    price = session_levels.get("current_price")

    # Get bias from basic analysis to orient OTE direction
    basic = get_ict_analysis(bars, current_price=price)
    long_score = basic.get("long_setup", {}).get("score", 0)
    short_score = basic.get("short_setup", {}).get("score", 0)
    bias = "bullish" if long_score > short_score else "bearish" if short_score > long_score else "neutral"

    advanced = get_advanced_signals(
        bars=bars,
        session_levels=session_levels,
        equal_hl=equal_hl,
        bars_secondary=bars_secondary,
        bias_direction=bias,
    )

    return {
        "symbol": symbol,
        "secondary": secondary,
        "bias_direction": bias,
        "current_price": price,
        **advanced,
    }

content.content:
from providers.yahoo import get_qqq_price, get_vwap, get_leadership_quotes, get_news, get_intraday
from engines.bias import BiasEngine
from engines.stats import calc_wave, calc_gex, calc_top_flow, calc_unusual
from engines.ict import get_session_context
from providers.yahoo import get_options_chain

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/market", tags=["market"])
bias_engine = BiasEngine()

_cache: dict = {}


def _fresh_options():
    chain = get_options_chain("QQQ")
    return chain.get("calls", []), chain.get("puts", [])


@router.get("/overview")
async def market_overview():
    try:
        price_data = get_qqq_price()
        vwap = get_vwap("QQQ")
        leadership = get_leadership_quotes()
        calls, puts = _fresh_options()
        wave = calc_wave(calls, puts)
        price = price_data.get("price") or 450.0
        gex_data = calc_gex(calls, puts, price)
        session = get_session_context()

        market_data = {
            "qqq_price": price,
            "qqq_change_pct": price_data.get("change_pct"),
            "vwap": vwap,
            "wave": wave,
            "leadership": leadership,
            "call_wall": gex_data.get("call_wall"),
            "put_wall": gex_data.get("put_wall"),
        }
        bias = bias_engine.calculate(market_data)
        green_count = sum(1 for s in leadership if s.get("bullish"))

        return {
            "price": price_data,
            "vwap": vwap,
            "vwap_status": "above" if (price and vwap and price > vwap) else "below",
            "bias": bias,
            "wave_summary": wave,
            "leadership_summary": {
                "green": green_count,
                "total": len(leadership),
                "breadth_pct": round(green_count / len(leadership) * 100) if leadership else 0,
--
@router.get("/news")
async def market_news():
    try:
        return {"news": get_news("QQQ")}
    except Exception as e:
        return {"error": str(e)}

The yfinance news API changed — it now returns `{'id': ..., 'content': {...}}` instead of the old flat format. I need to update the `get_news()` function to handle the new format.

The new format is:
```python
{
  'id': '...',
  'content': {
    'title': '...',
    'pubDate': '2026-05-16T22:20:01Z',
    'provider': {'displayName': 'Motley Fool', 'url': '...'},
    'canonicalUrl': {'url': 'https://...'},
    'clickThroughUrl': {'url': 'https://...'},
    'summary': '...',
    ...
  }
}
```

So I need to update the parser to use `item.get('content', {})` instead of accessing the old flat keys. I've also got a few other tasks to tackle: fixing the currency formatting in the API, updating the news function to pull articles for multiple tickers, adding an ICT decision panel, and looking into the Chrome browser settings for VS Code — I think it's under Settings where you can configure the default browser for opening links.

For the Chrome question specifically, in Codespaces the ports open in whatever browser is currently active, so the user might need to set Chrome as their default in Windows settings or manually copy the URL from the Ports tab.

Now I'm refactoring the news function to handle multiple tickers and filter out duplicates based on title, while also adding sentiment analysis using keyword matching to categorize articles as bullish or bearish.

Fixing the code issues... Extracting the publication timestamp from either the content's pubDate or displayTime fields, falling back to a Unix timestamp conversion if needed, then analyzing the title against bullish and bearish sentiment keywords to determine market sentiment.

Adding the currency formatter to the API utilities...

Now I'm working on the ICT decision panel component that will pull together all the signal analysis. For the Chrome issue in VS Code, the user needs to either set Chrome as their default browser in Windows or configure the `window.customExternalBrowser` setting in VS Code to point to Chrome directly—that should handle the port preview behavior in Codespace.

For the decision logic itself, I'm setting up the conditions: a strong long signal fires when we're in the killzone with a recent bullish sweep, discounted price, upward DOL direction, and a bullish iFVG present. The inverse applies for shorts. If we're in the killzone but haven't seen a sweep yet, or if we've detected a sweep outside the iFVG zone, we wait. We skip trading entirely if we're outside the killzone, the signals are neutral, or there's no DOL data.

def get_news(ticker: str = "QQQ") -> list:
    SENTIMENT_KEYWORDS = {
        "bullish": ["surge", "rally", "gain", "rise", "beat", "record", "high", "upgrade", "buy", "positive", "strong", "bullish"],
        "bearish": ["drop", "fall", "decline", "loss", "miss", "low", "downgrade", "sell", "negative", "crash", "recession", "weak", "bearish", "slump"],
    }
    # Pull news from multiple relevant tickers and merge
    tickers_to_fetch = ["QQQ", "NVDA", "MSFT", "SPY"] if ticker in ("QQQ", "SPY") else [ticker]
    seen, results = set(), []

    for tick in tickers_to_fetch:
        try:
            raw = yf.Ticker(tick).news or []
            for item in raw[:12]:
                # yfinance v0.2.50+ nests everything under 'content'
                content = item.get("content") or item
                title = content.get("title") or item.get("title", "")
                if not title or title in seen:
                    continue
                seen.add(title)

                # Link: prefer canonicalUrl, fall back to clickThroughUrl / link
                link = (
                    (content.get("canonicalUrl") or {}).get("url")
                    or (content.get("clickThroughUrl") or {}).get("url")
                    or item.get("link", "")
                )
                # Publisher
                publisher = (
                    (content.get("provider") or {}).get("displayName")
                    or item.get("publisher", "")
                )
                # Timestamp: ISO string from new API or Unix int from old API
                ts_raw = content.get("pubDate") or content.get("displayTime")
                if ts_raw:
                    timestamp = ts_raw
                else:
                    pub_time = item.get("providerPublishTime", 0)
                    timestamp = datetime.fromtimestamp(pub_time, tz=timezone.utc).isoformat() if pub_time else None

                # Sentiment scoring
                title_lower = title.lower()
                bull = sum(1 for w in SENTIMENT_KEYWORDS["bullish"] if w in title_lower)
                bear = sum(1 for w in SENTIMENT_KEYWORDS["bearish"] if w in title_lower)
                sentiment = "bullish" if bull > bear else "bearish" if bear > bull else "neutral"

                results.append({
                    "title": title,
                    "publisher": publisher,
                    "link": link,
                    "timestamp": timestamp,
                    "sentiment": sentiment,
                    "summary": content.get("summary", ""),
                    "ticker": tick,
                })
        except Exception:
            continue

    # Sort newest first, cap at 25
    results.sort(key=lambda x: x.get("timestamp") or "", reverse=True)
    return results[:25]

content.text:
Done. Here's what changed:

**Risk Manager** — `fmt.currency` now exists. The crash is fixed and won't bleed into other tabs anymore.

**News** — Yahoo changed their API format (titles were buried under `content.title` now). Fixed, pulls from QQQ + NVDA + MSFT + SPY, deduplicates, up to 25 articles sorted newest first.

**ICT Decision Panel** — sits below PO3 phase, above the sweep alert. It scores every signal (killzone, sweep, discount/premium, draw on liquidity, iFVG, OTE, structure, SMT) and outputs:
- **POTENTIAL LONG / SHORT** with confidence %, specific action ("wait for iFVG at 468.20–469.10"), and invalidation condition
- **WAIT — No Sweep Yet** when in killzone but nothing happened yet
- **NO TRADE** when outside killzone or mixed signals

On the data question — **yes, exactly right**. Weekend + Yahoo = limited intraday bars, so IPDA/OTE needs more history. Monday open will fix most of it. Schwab with extended hours will make it significantly better (Asia/London bars, 60+ days of history).

**For Chrome** — in VS Code, go to Settings (`Ctrl+,`), search `external browser`, and set it to your Chrome path (e.g. `C:\Program Files\Google\Chrome\Application\chrome.exe`). Or the faster way: just copy the port URL from the **Ports** tab and paste it into Chrome manually.

The user has several feedback points:

1. **EOD trailing drawdown** - The trailing drawdown is end-of-day (EOD), not intraday. So the "session high" concept doesn't apply. The floor is calculated based on the closing balance at end of each day, not the intraday high. So if you start at $25,000, your floor is $24,000. If you close day 1 at $25,400, your new floor is $24,400. The floor only moves at EOD, not during the day.

2. **$300 risk per trade is too much** - The user makes a good point. With only $1,000 daily loss limit to start, risking $300 per trade means only 3 losing trades in a row blow the day. 

I'm working through what a more reasonable risk per trade would be. Looking at it as a percentage of the daily loss limit, something in the 10-15% range seems more balanced—that gives me enough room for multiple losing trades before hitting the daily cap, while still being meaningful per trade. Actually, for a funded account with a $1,000 daily loss limit, a common approach is:
- Risk 1-2% of account per trade = $250-$500 (too much for starting)
- Risk no more than 20-25% of daily limit per trade = $200-$250
- This gives you 4-5 attempts in a day before you're done

But given the user said "maybe 300 is too much", I think $150-$200 is more reasonable for the start. Maybe default to $150 (15% of daily limit = ~6 attempts) and let it scale as buffer builds.

Good catches on all three. Let me address each before touching code:

1. **EOD trailing** — you're right. The floor resets based on yesterday's *close*, not intraday peak. I'll swap "session high" for "prev close balance"
2. **$300 risk** — too aggressive to start. I'll default to $150 max per trade (gives you 6+ attempts before limit). Scales up as buffer builds
3. **Auto sizing from ICT** — this is the right call. The system sees the sweep, the iFVG, the DOL target — it should just tell you: "2 MNQ contracts, entry 468.50, stop 467.20, target 472.10 (2.4R)"

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user (Diego) is building a professional NASDAQ Flow Terminal for trading MNQ/NQ futures using ICT/SMC concepts. This session started after losing a previous session (accidental image send caused chat errors). The user pasted the full previous session transcript in 3 parts to restore context.

   **Explicit requests completed this session:**
   - Restore context from previous session transcript
   - Build missing files that couldn't be pushed: `risk.py`, `ict_signals.py`, `routes/risk.py`, `Risk.jsx`
   - Wire advanced ICT signals (MSS/CHoCH, OTE, IPDA, SMT, PO3) into the ICT page UI
   - Add a "confluence decision" panel to ICT page that synthesizes all signals into a clear trade recommendation
   - Fix Risk Manager crash (`fmt.currency is not a function`)
   - Fix News page (yfinance API format changed), show 25+ articles
   - Confirm insufficient IPDA/OTE data is a weekend/Yahoo limitation, not a bug
   - Push all commits to GitHub

   **Explicit requests PENDING (cut off mid-work):**
   - Fix EOD trailing drawdown (not intraday session high)
   - Reduce default risk per trade from $300 to $150
   - Auto-calculate position sizing from ICT signals (entry, stop, target, contracts all auto)

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - **ICT Sweep & Inverse Model**: Mark session levels (Asia/London/NY H/L), wait for sweep, find FVG on 30s/1m/2m/3m, enter on iFVG inversion targeting opposing draw on liquidity
   - **PO3/AMD Phase**: Accumulation → Manipulation (sweep) → Distribution (delivery)
   - **OTE Zone**: 0.618–0.786 Fibonacci retracement of most recent impulse leg
   - **IPDA Levels**: 20/40/60 trading-day rolling highs/lows (price is drawn to unvisited IPDA levels)
   - **SMT Divergence**: QQQ vs SPY non-confirmation at swing extremes (reversal signal)
   - **MSS/CHoCH**: Market Structure Shift / Change of Character (structure analysis)
   - **Lucid 25k Pro Rules**: $25k account, $1k EOD trailing drawdown, floor locks at $25,100, payout at $26,100, safe trigger at $28,000
   - **Trailing Floor**: EOD-based (not intraday) — floor = yesterday's close - $1,000, capped at $25,100
   - **Instruments**: MNQ ($2/pt Micro NQ), MES ($5/pt Micro ES), MGC ($10/pt Micro Gold)
   - **QQQ→MNQ conversion**: 1 QQQ point ≈ 44–45 MNQ points (NDX/QQQ ratio ≈ 44x)
   - **NY Killzone**: 9:30–11:30 AM ET (first 45 min is highest probability)
   - **Schwab API**: OAuth2-based, production callback URL updates after market hours

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - **`backend/engines/risk.py`** (CREATED) — Lucid 25k Pro prop firm math
     - Currently uses `highest_balance` (intraday) — needs to change to `prev_close_balance` (EOD)
     - `calc_account_status(current_balance, highest_balance=None)` → needs rename
     - `size_position(instrument, stop_points, account_status, risk_per_trade_pct=0.3, max_risk_per_trade=300)` → needs $150 cap
     - INSTRUMENTS: `MNQ: $2/pt`, `MES: $5/pt`, `MGC: $10/pt`
     - Constants: STARTING_BALANCE=25000, DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT=1000, TRAIL_FLOOR_STOPS_AT=25100, PAYOUT_THRESHOLD=26100, MAX_PAYOUT=1500, SAFE_PAYOUT_TRIGGER=28000

   - **`backend/engines/ict_signals.py`** (CREATED) — Advanced ICT signals
     - `detect_swings(bars, lookback=3)` — pivot highs/lows
     - `detect_mss_choch(bars, swings)` — structure shift detection
     - `detect_liquidity_sweeps(bars, session_levels, equal_hl)` — wick-through + body closes back
     - `calc_ote_zone(bars, swings, direction)` — 0.618–0.786 Fib
     - `calc_ipda_levels(bars)` — 20/40/60 day rolling levels
     - `detect_smt_divergence(bars_primary, bars_secondary, lookback=10)` — non-confirmation
     - `detect_po3_phase(bars, session_levels)` — AMD phase detection
     - `get_advanced_signals(bars, session_levels, equal_hl, bars_secondary, bias_direction)` — combined
     - **MISSING**: `calc_auto_trade_setup()` — needs to be added for auto position sizing

   - **`backend/routes/risk.py`** (CREATED) — Risk API endpoints
     - `GET /api/risk/account?balance=&highest=` — currently uses "highest" param, needs "prev_close"
     - `GET /api/risk/size?instrument=&stop_points=&balance=&highest=`
     - `GET /api/risk/instruments`
     - `GET /api/risk/payout`

   - **`backend/routes/ict.py`** (MODIFIED) — Added advanced endpoint
     ```python
     from engines.ict_signals import get_advanced_signals
     
     @router.get("/advanced")
     async def advanced_analysis(symbol: str = Query("QQQ"), secondary: str = Query("SPY")):
         bars = await _get_bars(symbol)
         bars_secondary = await _get_bars(secondary)
         session_levels = extract_session_levels(bars)
         equal_hl = detect_equal_highs_lows(bars)
         price = session_levels.get("current_price")
         basic = get_ict_analysis(bars, current_price=price)
         long_score = basic.get("long_setup", {}).get("score", 0)
     ```

file.content:
"""ICT / Smart Money analysis routes."""
from fastapi import APIRouter, Query
from engines.ict import (get_ict_analysis, get_session_context,
                          detect_fvg, extract_session_levels,
                          calc_discount_premium, identify_draw_on_liquidity,
                          detect_equal_highs_lows)
from engines.ict_signals import get_advanced_signals
from providers import yahoo, schwab as schwab_provider

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/ict", tags=["ict"])


async def _get_bars(symbol: str = "QQQ") -> list:
    if schwab_provider.has_tokens():
        try:
            data = await schwab_provider.get_price_history(
                symbol, period_type="day", period=2,
                frequency_type="minute", frequency=5, extended_hours=True,
            )
            if data and data.get("candles"):
                import datetime
                bars = []
                for c in data["candles"]:
                    dt = datetime.datetime.fromtimestamp(c["datetime"] / 1000, tz=datetime.timezone.utc)
                    bars.append({"time": dt.isoformat(), "open": c.get("open"),
                                 "high": c.get("high"), "low": c.get("low"),
                                 "close": c.get("close"), "volume": c.get("volume")})
                return bars
        except Exception:
            pass
    return yahoo.get_intraday(symbol, interval="5m")


@router.get("/analysis")
async def ict_analysis(symbol: str = Query("QQQ")):
    bars = await _get_bars(symbol)
    vwap = yahoo.get_vwap(symbol)
    price = bars[-1]["close"] if bars and bars[-1].get("close") else None
    return get_ict_analysis(bars, vwap=vwap, current_price=price)


@router.get("/session")
async def session_context():
    return get_session_context()


@router.get("/fvgs")
async def fvgs(symbol: str = Query("QQQ")):
    bars = await _get_bars(symbol)
    return {"symbol": symbol, "fvgs": detect_fvg(bars), "bar_count": len(bars)}


@router.get("/levels")
async def session_levels(symbol: str = Query("QQQ")):
    bars = await _get_bars(symbol)
    levels = extract_session_levels(bars)
    price = levels.get("current_price")
    equal_hl = detect_equal_highs_lows(bars)
    return {
        "symbol": symbol, "levels": levels,
        "discount_premium": calc_discount_premium(levels, price),
        "draw_on_liquidity": identify_draw_on_liquidity(levels, equal_hl, price),
        "equal_highs_lows": equal_hl,
    }


@router.get("/advanced")
async def advanced_analysis(symbol: str = Query("QQQ"), secondary: str = Query("SPY")):
    """Advanced ICT signals: MSS/CHoCH, sweeps, OTE, IPDA, SMT, PO3."""
    bars = await _get_bars(symbol)
    bars_secondary = await _get_bars(secondary)

    session_levels = extract_session_levels(bars)
    equal_hl = detect_equal_highs_lows(bars)
    price = session_levels.get("current_price")

    # Get bias from basic analysis to orient OTE direction
    basic = get_ict_analysis(bars, current_price=price)
    long_score = basic.get("long_setup", {}).get("score", 0)
    short_score = basic.get("short_setup", {}).get("score", 0)
    bias = "bullish" if long_score > short_score else "bearish" if short_score > long_score else "neutral"

    advanced = get_advanced_signals(
        bars=bars,
        session_levels=session_levels,
        equal_hl=equal_hl,
        bars_secondary=bars_secondary,
        bias_direction=bias,
    )

    return {
        "symbol": symbol,
        "secondary": secondary,
        "bias_direction": bias,
        "current_price": price,
        **advanced,
    }

description: How Diego communicates and what to do/avoid when working with him
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: feedback
  originSessionId: 6d7a8786-ed73-4d22-8f36-b01b0ee5c497
---

- Diego types casually and fast — don't over-interpret typos or abbreviations
- He pastes long transcripts and screenshots to share context — read them fully before asking questions
- He bounces between home desktop and work Codespace — always commit before doing big changes, and remind him to `git push` before leaving a machine
- Don't ask too many questions before starting — he prefers action over planning discussions
- When session context is lost, read the codebase directly rather than asking him to re-explain everything
- He works on one big feature at a time; don't suggest scope creep mid-task
- The Schwab API credentials are in backend/.env — never commit them to git (already in .gitignore)

**Why:** Diego lost a full session of work when the chat errored out from an accidental image send. Saving memory and committing frequently prevents repeating that pain.

"""Risk management API routes — prop firm math + position sizing."""
from fastapi import APIRouter, Query
from engines.risk import calc_account_status, size_position, daily_trade_plan, INSTRUMENTS

router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/risk", tags=["risk"])


@router.get("/account")
async def account_status(
    balance: float = Query(25000.0, description="Current account balance"),
    highest: float = Query(None, description="Session highest balance (defaults to balance)"),
):
    status = calc_account_status(balance, highest)
    plan = daily_trade_plan(status)
    return {**status, "trade_plan": plan}


@router.get("/size")
async def position_size(
    instrument: str = Query("MNQ", description="MNQ, MES, or MGC"),
    stop_points: float = Query(..., description="Stop distance in points"),
    balance: float = Query(25000.0),
    highest: float = Query(None),
    risk_pct: float = Query(0.3, description="Fraction of daily risk remaining to use per trade"),
    max_risk: float = Query(300.0, description="Absolute max risk per trade in dollars"),
):
    status = calc_account_status(balance, highest)
    sizing = size_position(
        instrument=instrument,
        stop_points=stop_points,
        account_status=status,
        risk_per_trade_pct=risk_pct,
        max_risk_per_trade=max_risk,
    )
    return {
        "account": {
            "balance": status["current_balance"],
            "daily_risk_remaining": status["daily_risk_remaining"],
            "status": status["status"],
        },
        "sizing": sizing,
    }


@router.get("/instruments")
async def instruments():
    return {
        k: {
            **v,
            "example_stop_5pt_risk": round(5 * v["dollars_per_point"], 2),
            "example_stop_10pt_risk": round(10 * v["dollars_per_point"], 2),
        }
        for k, v in INSTRUMENTS.items()
    }


@router.get("/payout")
async def payout_status(
    balance: float = Query(25000.0),
    highest: float = Query(None),
):
    status = calc_account_status(balance, highest)
    return {
        "current_balance": status["current_balance"],
        "payout_ready": status["payout_ready"],
        "payout_threshold": status["payout_threshold"],
        "to_threshold": status["to_payout_threshold"],
        "max_payout": status["max_payout"],
        "safe_trigger": status["safe_payout_trigger"],
        "to_safe_trigger": status["to_safe_trigger"],
        "safe_after_payout": status["safe_after_payout"],
        "days_at_300": status["days_at_300"],
        "days_at_400": status["days_at_400"],
        "days_at_500": status["days_at_500"],
        "message": (
            "Ready to take $1,500 payout!" if status["payout_ready"] and status["current_balance"] >= status["safe_payout_trigger"]
            else f"Need ${status['to_safe_trigger']:.0f} more for safe payout trigger"
            if not status["payout_ready"] else
            f"Above threshold — need ${status['to_safe_trigger']:.0f} more for safe payout buffer"
        ),
    }

"""Prop firm risk engine — Lucid 25k Pro rules + position sizing."""
from typing import Optional

# Instrument specs: dollars per point
INSTRUMENTS = {
    "MNQ": {"name": "Micro NQ", "dollars_per_point": 2.0, "proxy": "QQQ", "session": "NY"},
    "MES": {"name": "Micro ES", "dollars_per_point": 5.0, "proxy": "SPY", "session": "NY"},
    "MGC": {"name": "Micro Gold", "dollars_per_point": 10.0, "proxy": "GC=F", "session": "Asia"},
}

# Lucid 25k Pro constants
STARTING_BALANCE = 25_000.0
DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT = 1_000.0
TRAIL_FLOOR_STOPS_AT = 25_100.0   # floor stops trailing once it reaches this
PAYOUT_THRESHOLD = 26_100.0       # minimum balance to request payout
MAX_PAYOUT = 1_500.0
SAFE_AFTER_PAYOUT = 26_500.0      # balance must stay above this after payout
SAFE_PAYOUT_TRIGGER = SAFE_AFTER_PAYOUT + MAX_PAYOUT  # 28,000


def calc_trailing_floor(highest_balance: float) -> float:
    """Trailing max-loss floor. Follows balance up but never exceeds TRAIL_FLOOR_STOPS_AT."""
    floor = highest_balance - DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT
    return min(floor, TRAIL_FLOOR_STOPS_AT)


def calc_account_status(current_balance: float, highest_balance: Optional[float] = None) -> dict:
    """
    Full account status for the risk manager.

    Args:
        current_balance: Current P&L-adjusted balance
        highest_balance: Session high (defaults to current_balance if not provided)
    """
    if highest_balance is None:
        highest_balance = current_balance

    floor = calc_trailing_floor(highest_balance)
    daily_risk_remaining = current_balance - floor
    daily_pnl = current_balance - STARTING_BALANCE

    # How far from payout target
    to_payout = max(0.0, PAYOUT_THRESHOLD - current_balance)
    to_safe_trigger = max(0.0, SAFE_PAYOUT_TRIGGER - current_balance)
    payout_ready = current_balance >= PAYOUT_THRESHOLD

    # Days to build buffer at various daily targets
    def days_needed(target_per_day: float) -> Optional[int]:
        if target_per_day <= 0 or current_balance >= SAFE_PAYOUT_TRIGGER:
            return 0
        needed = SAFE_PAYOUT_TRIGGER - current_balance
        return max(1, round(needed / target_per_day))

    # Status label
    if current_balance <= floor:
        status = "BLOWN"
        status_color = "red"
    elif daily_risk_remaining < DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT * 0.25:
        status = "DANGER"
        status_color = "red"
    elif daily_risk_remaining < DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT * 0.5:
        status = "CAUTION"
        status_color = "yellow"
    elif payout_ready and current_balance >= SAFE_PAYOUT_TRIGGER:
        status = "PAYOUT READY"
        status_color = "green"
    elif payout_ready:
        status = "ABOVE THRESHOLD"
        status_color = "blue"
    else:
        status = "BUILDING"
        status_color = "green"

    return {
        "current_balance": round(current_balance, 2),
        "highest_balance": round(highest_balance, 2),
        "trailing_floor": round(floor, 2),
        "daily_pnl": round(daily_pnl, 2),
        "daily_risk_remaining": round(daily_risk_remaining, 2),
        "daily_risk_used": round(DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT - daily_risk_remaining, 2),
        "daily_risk_pct_used": round((DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT - daily_risk_remaining) / DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT * 100, 1),
        "floor_trailing": floor < TRAIL_FLOOR_STOPS_AT,
        "floor_locked": floor >= TRAIL_FLOOR_STOPS_AT,
        "payout_ready": payout_ready,
        "payout_threshold": PAYOUT_THRESHOLD,
        "safe_payout_trigger": SAFE_PAYOUT_TRIGGER,
        "to_payout_threshold": round(to_payout, 2),
        "to_safe_trigger": round(to_safe_trigger, 2),
        "max_payout": MAX_PAYOUT,
        "safe_after_payout": SAFE_AFTER_PAYOUT,
        "days_at_300": days_needed(300),
        "days_at_400": days_needed(400),
        "days_at_500": days_needed(500),
        "status": status,
        "status_color": status_color,
        "rules": {
            "starting_balance": STARTING_BALANCE,
            "daily_loss_limit": DAILY_LOSS_LIMIT,
            "trail_stops_at": TRAIL_FLOOR_STOPS_AT,
            "payout_threshold": PAYOUT_THRESHOLD,
            "max_payout": MAX_PAYOUT,
        }
    }


def size_position(
    instrument: str,
    stop_points: float,
    account_status: dict,
    risk_per_trade_pct: float = 0.3,  # % of daily risk remaining per trade
    max_risk_per_trade: float = 300.0,
) -> dict:
    """
    Calculate position size for a given instrument and stop distance.

    Args:
        instrument: MNQ, MES, or MGC
        stop_points: Distance from entry to stop loss in points
        account_status: Output of calc_account_status()
        risk_per_trade_pct: Fraction of daily_risk_remaining to risk per trade
        max_risk_per_trade: Absolute cap on per-trade risk
    """
    spec = INSTRUMENTS.get(instrument.upper())
    if not spec or stop_points <= 0:
        return {"error": "Invalid instrument or stop distance"}

    daily_risk_remaining = account_status.get("daily_risk_remaining", 0)
    if daily_risk_remaining <= 0:
        return {
            "instrument": instrument,
            "contracts": 0,
            "risk_amount": 0,
            "note": "No daily risk remaining — stop trading today.",
        }

    # Risk amount for this trade
    risk_amount = min(
        daily_risk_remaining * risk_per_trade_pct,
        max_risk_per_trade,
        daily_risk_remaining * 0.5,   # never more than 50% of remaining in one trade
    )

    dollars_per_point = spec["dollars_per_point"]
    risk_per_contract = stop_points * dollars_per_point
    contracts = max(1, int(risk_amount / risk_per_contract)) if risk_per_contract > 0 else 1

    actual_risk = contracts * risk_per_contract

    # TP levels
    tp_levels = {}
    for r, label in [(1.5, "1.5R"), (2.0, "2R"), (2.5, "2.5R"), (3.0, "3R")]:
        tp_pts = round(stop_points * r, 2)
        tp_gain = round(tp_pts * dollars_per_point * contracts, 2)
        tp_levels[label] = {"points": tp_pts, "gain": tp_gain}

    return {
        "instrument": instrument.upper(),
        "instrument_name": spec["name"],
        "dollars_per_point": dollars_per_point,
        "stop_points": stop_points,
        "risk_per_contract": round(risk_per_contract, 2),
        "risk_amount": round(actual_risk, 2),
        "contracts": contracts,
        "tp_levels": tp_levels,
        "note": f"{contracts} contract{'s' if contracts != 1 else ''} · ${actual_risk:.0f} risk · stop {stop_points} pts",
        "daily_risk_after_trade": round(daily_risk_remaining - actual_risk, 2),
    }


def daily_trade_plan(account_status: dict, instrument: str = "MNQ") -> dict:
    """Generate a simple daily trade plan based on account state."""
    remaining = account_status.get("daily_risk_remaining", 0)
    status = account_status.get("status", "")
    to_trigger = account_status.get("to_safe_trigger", 0)

    if status == "BLOWN":
        return {"recommendation": "STOP — daily loss limit hit. No more trades today.", "max_trades": 0}
    if status == "DANGER":
        return {"recommendation": "Extreme caution. 1 trade max. Reduce size by 50%.", "max_trades": 1}

    # How many trades at standard size can we take?
    risk_per_trade = min(remaining * 0.3, 300)
    max_trades = int(remaining / risk_per_trade) if risk_per_trade > 0 else 0
    daily_goal = min(300, to_trigger) if to_trigger > 0 else 300

    return {
        "recommendation": f"Target ${daily_goal:.0f} today. Max {min(max_trades, 3)} high-quality trades.",
        "max_trades": min(max_trades, 3),
        "risk_per_trade": round(risk_per_trade, 2),
        "daily_goal": daily_goal,
        "primary_instrument": instrument,
        "note": "Only take A+ ICT setups. Killzone only. Stop when daily goal hit.",
    }

2	from typing import Optional

9	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
10	# Swing point detection
11	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
12	
13	def detect_swings(bars: list, lookback: int = 3) -> dict:
14	    """Identify pivot highs and pivot lows."""
15	    highs, lows = [], []
16	    n = len(bars)
17	    for i in range(lookback, n - lookback):
18	        h = bars[i].get("high")
19	        l = bars[i].get("low")
20	        if h is None or l is None:
21	            continue
22	        # Swing high: highest in window
23	        window_highs = [bars[j].get("high", 0) for j in range(i - lookback, i + lookback + 1) if j != i]
24	        if all(h >= wh for wh in window_highs if wh):
25	            highs.append({"index": i, "price": round(h, 2), "time": bars[i].get("time")})
26	        # Swing low
27	        window_lows = [bars[j].get("low", float("inf")) for j in range(i - lookback, i + lookback + 1) if j != i]
28	        if all(l <= wl for wl in window_lows if wl != float("inf")):
29	            lows.append({"index": i, "price": round(l, 2), "time": bars[i].get("time")})
30	    return {"swing_highs": highs[-10:], "swing_lows": lows[-10:]}
31	
32	
33	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
34	# MSS / CHoCH detection
35	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
36	
37	def detect_mss_choch(bars: list, swings: Optional[dict] = None) -> dict:
38	    """
39	    Detect Market Structure Shift and Change of Character.
40	
41	    CHoCH: price closes beyond a recent swing in opposite direction (lower TF signal)
42	    MSS: CHoCH confirmed with strong displacement (higher confidence reversal)
43	    """
44	    if swings is None:
45	        swings = detect_swings(bars)
46	
47	    swing_highs = swings.get("swing_highs", [])
48	    swing_lows = swings.get("swing_lows", [])
49	    events = []
50	
51	    if len(bars) < 5 or not swing_highs or not swing_lows:
52	        return {"events": events, "last_structure": "unknown", "bias": "neutral"}
53	
54	    # Look at the most recent swing high and low
55	    last_high = swing_highs[-1] if swing_highs else None
56	    last_low = swing_lows[-1] if swing_lows else None
57	
58	    # Check recent bars for structure breaks
59	    for i in range(max(1, len(bars) - 20), len(bars)):
60	        b = bars[i]
61	        close = b.get("close")
62	        if close is None:
63	            continue
64	
65	        # Bearish CHoCH: close below the most recent swing low
66	        if last_low and close < last_low["price"] and i > last_low["index"]:
67	            prev_bar = bars[i - 1]
68	            displacement = abs(close - (prev_bar.get("open") or close))
69	            is_mss = displacement > 0.3  # strong displacement = MSS
70	            events.append({
71	                "type": "MSS_bearish" if is_mss else "CHoCH_bearish",
72	                "label": "MSS ↓" if is_mss else "CHoCH ↓",
73	                "price": round(close, 2),
74	                "broken_level": last_low["price"],
75	                "time": b.get("time"),
76	                "description": (
77	                    f"Bearish MSS — strong close below swing low {last_low['price']:.2f}. "
78	                    f"Structure shifted bearish." if is_mss else
79	                    f"Bearish CHoCH — close below swing low {last_low['price']:.2f}. "
80	                    f"Watch for distribution."
81	                ),
82	                "color": "red",
83	            })
84	
85	        # Bullish CHoCH: close above the most recent swing high
86	        if last_high and close > last_high["price"] and i > last_high["index"]:
87	            prev_bar = bars[i - 1]
88	            displacement = abs(close - (prev_bar.get("open") or close))
89	            is_mss = displacement > 0.3
90	            events.append({
91	                "type": "MSS_bullish" if is_mss else "CHoCH_bullish",
92	                "label": "MSS ↑" if is_mss else "CHoCH ↑",
93	                "price": round(close, 2),
94	                "broken_level": last_high["price"],
95	                "time": b.get("time"),
96	                "description": (
97	                    f"Bullish MSS — strong close above swing high {last_high['price']:.2f}. "
98	                    f"Structure shifted bullish." if is_mss else
99	                    f"Bullish CHoCH — close above swing high {last_high['price']:.2f}. "
100	                    f"Watch for accumulation."
101	                ),
102	                "color": "green",
103	            })
104	
105	    # Overall structure bias from last few swings
106	    last_structure = "neutral"
107	    if len(swing_highs) >= 2 and len(swing_lows) >= 2:
108	        hh = swing_highs[-1]["price"] > swing_highs[-2]["price"]
109	        hl = swing_lows[-1]["price"] > swing_lows[-2]["price"]
110	        lh = swing_highs[-1]["price"] < swing_highs[-2]["price"]
111	        ll = swing_lows[-1]["price"] < swing_lows[-2]["price"]
112	        if hh and hl:
113	            last_structure = "bullish"
114	        elif lh and ll:
115	            last_structure = "bearish"
116	        elif hh or hl:
117	            last_structure = "bullish_lean"
118	        elif lh or ll:
119	            last_structure = "bearish_lean"
120	
121	    return {
122	        "events": events[-5:],
123	        "last_structure": last_structure,
124	        "bias": last_structure,
125	    }

128	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
129	# Liquidity sweep detector
130	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
131	
132	def detect_liquidity_sweeps(bars: list, session_levels: dict, equal_hl: Optional[dict] = None) -> list:
133	    """
134	    Detect when price wicked through a key level but closed back inside (sweep/stop hunt).
135	    """
136	    sweeps = []
137	    if len(bars) < 3:
138	        return sweeps
139	
140	    # Build level list to check
141	    levels = []
142	    for key, label in [
143	        ("asia_high", "Asia High"), ("asia_low", "Asia Low"),
144	        ("london_high", "London High"), ("london_low", "London Low"),
145	        ("premarket_high", "Pre-Market High"), ("premarket_low", "Pre-Market Low"),
146	        ("prev_day_high", "Prev Day High"), ("prev_day_low", "Prev Day Low"),
147	        ("today_high", "Today's High"), ("today_low", "Today's Low"),
148	    ]:
149	        v = session_levels.get(key)
150	        if v:
151	            levels.append({"level": v, "label": label, "is_high": "high" in key})
152	
153	    if equal_hl:
154	        for eh in equal_hl.get("equal_highs", []):
155	            levels.append({"level": eh["level"], "label": f"EQH {eh['level']:.2f}", "is_high": True})
156	        for el in equal_hl.get("equal_lows", []):
157	            levels.append({"level": el["level"], "label": f"EQL {el['level']:.2f}", "is_high": False})
158	
159	    # Check recent bars for wick-through + body-close-back
160	    for i in range(max(1, len(bars) - 30), len(bars)):
161	        b = bars[i]
162	        high, low = b.get("high"), b.get("low")
163	        open_, close = b.get("open"), b.get("close")
164	        if None in (high, low, open_, close):
165	            continue
166	
167	        body_high = max(open_, close)
168	        body_low = min(open_, close)
169	
170	        for lvl in levels:
171	            lv = lvl["level"]
172	            label = lvl["label"]
173	            is_high_level = lvl["is_high"]
174	
175	            # Bullish sweep: wick below level, body closes above
176	            if is_high_level is False and low < lv and body_low >= lv:
177	                sweeps.append({
178	                    "type": "bullish_sweep",
179	                    "label": f"Swept {label}",
180	                    "level": lv,
181	                    "wick_low": round(low, 2),
182	                    "close": round(close, 2),
183	                    "time": b.get("time"),
184	                    "description": (
185	                        f"Bullish sweep of {label} ({lv:.2f}) — wick below, closed above. "
186	                        f"Stop-hunt complete. Look for bullish iFVG entry targeting higher draws."
187	                    ),
188	                    "direction": "bullish",
189	                })
190	
191	            # Bearish sweep: wick above level, body closes below
192	            if is_high_level is True and high > lv and body_high <= lv:
193	                sweeps.append({
194	                    "type": "bearish_sweep",
195	                    "label": f"Swept {label}",
196	                    "level": lv,
197	                    "wick_high": round(high, 2),
198	                    "close": round(close, 2),
199	                    "time": b.get("time"),
200	                    "description": (
201	                        f"Bearish sweep of {label} ({lv:.2f}) — wick above, closed below. "
202	                        f"Stop-hunt complete. Look for bearish iFVG entry targeting lower draws."
203	                    ),
204	                    "direction": "bearish",
205	                })
206	
207	    return sweeps[-10:]
